title: Fimbriella lacera
intro: Fimbriella lacera, also known as "Cutleaf," is a type of medicinal plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Fimbriella lacera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Fimbriella lacera is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and flu, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory discomfort.
